name: FixedOrderComparator
javadoc:
/** * A Comparator which imposes a specific order on a specific set of Objects. * Objects are presented to the FixedOrderComparator in a specified order and * subsequent calls to {@link #compare(Object, Object) compare} yield that order. * For example: * 
 * String[] planets = {"Mercury", "Venus", "Earth", "Mars"};
 * FixedOrderComparator distanceFromSun = new FixedOrderComparator(planets);
 * Arrays.sort(planets);                     // Sort to alphabetical order
 * Arrays.sort(planets, distanceFromSun);    // Back to original order
 *
*

* Once compare has been called, the FixedOrderComparator is locked * and attempts to modify it yield an UnsupportedOperationException. *

* Instances of FixedOrderComparator are not synchronized. The class is not * thread-safe at construction time, but it is thread-safe to perform * multiple comparisons after all the setup operations are complete. *

* This class is Serializable from Commons Collections 4.0. * * @since 3.0 * @version $Id: FixedOrderComparator.java 1686855 2015-06-22 13:00:27Z tn $ */

members.name: FixedOrderComparator
FixedOrderComparator
members.javadoc:
/** * Constructs a FixedOrderComparator which uses the order of the given array * to compare the objects. *

* The array is copied, so later changes will not affect the comparator. * * @param items the items that the comparator can compare in order * @throws NullPointerException if the array is null */

tags.name: items
tags.content: the items that the comparator can compare in order
tags.description: the items that the comparator can compare in order

tags.name: NullPointerException
tags.content: if the array is null
tags.description: if the array is null

members.name: isLocked
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns true if modifications cannot be made to the FixedOrderComparator.
 * FixedOrderComparators cannot be modified once they have performed a comparison.
 *
 * @return true if attempts to change the FixedOrderComparator yield an
 *  UnsupportedOperationException, false if it can be changed.
 */

tags.content: if attempts to change the FixedOrderComparator yield an UnsupportedOperationException, false if it can be changed.
tags.description: true if attempts to change the FixedOrderComparator yield an UnsupportedOperationException, false if it can be changed.

 */

tags.content: if the comparator is locked

/**

 *
 * @param unknownObjectBehavior  the flag for unknown behaviour -
 * UNKNOWN_AFTER, UNKNOWN_BEFORE or UNKNOWN_THROW_EXCEPTION

members.name: add
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Adds an item, which compares as after all items known to the Comparator.
 * If the item is already known to the Comparator, its old position is
 * replaced with the new position.
 *
 * @param obj  the item to be added to the Comparator.
 * @return true if obj has been added for the first time, false if
 *  it was already known to the Comparator.
 * @throws UnsupportedOperationException if a comparison has already been made
 */

tags.name: obj
tags.content: the item to be added to the Comparator.
tags.description: the item to be added to the Comparator.

tags.content: if obj has been added for the first time, false if it was already known to the Comparator.
tags.description: true if obj has been added for the first time, false if it was already known to the Comparator.

tags.name: UnsupportedOperationException
tags.content: if a comparison has already been made
tags.description: if a comparison has already been made

members.name: addAsEqual
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Adds a new item, which compares as equal to the given existing item.
 *
 * @param existingObj  an item already in the Comparator's set of
 *  known objects
 * @param newObj  an item to be added to the Comparator's set of
 *  known objects
 * @return true if newObj has been added for the first time, false if
 *  it was already known to the Comparator.
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if existingObject is not in the
 *  Comparator's set of known objects.
 * @throws UnsupportedOperationException if a comparison has already been made
 */

tags.name: existingObj
tags.content: an item already in the Comparator's set of known objects
tags.description: an item already in the Comparator's set of known objects

tags.name: newObj
tags.content: an item to be added to the Comparator's set of known objects
tags.description: an item to be added to the Comparator's set of known objects

tags.content: if newObj has been added for the first time, false if it was already known to the Comparator.
tags.description: true if newObj has been added for the first time, false if it was already known to the Comparator.

tags.name: IllegalArgumentException
tags.content: if existingObject is not in the Comparator's set of known objects.
tags.description: if existingObject is not in the Comparator's set of known objects.

tags.name: UnsupportedOperationException
tags.content: if a comparison has already been made
tags.description: if a comparison has already been made

members.name: compare
members.javadoc:
/** * Compares two objects according to the order of this Comparator. *

* It is important to note that this class will throw an IllegalArgumentException * in the case of an unrecognised object. This is not specified in the * Comparator interface, but is the most appropriate exception. * * @param obj1 the first object to compare * @param obj2 the second object to compare * @return negative if obj1 is less, positive if greater, zero if equal * @throws IllegalArgumentException if obj1 or obj2 are not known * to this Comparator and an alternative behavior has not been set * via {@link #setUnknownObjectBehavior(UnknownObjectBehavior)}. */

tags.name: obj1
tags.content: the first object to compare
tags.description: the first object to compare

tags.name: obj2
tags.content: the second object to compare
tags.description: the second object to compare

tags.content: if obj1 is less, positive if greater, zero if equal
tags.assertion: assert methodResultID < 0 || methodResultID > 0 || methodResultID == 0 : "methodResultID must be negative if obj1 is less, positive if obj1 is greater, or zero if obj1 is equal to obj2";
tags.description: negative if obj1 is less, positive if greater, zero if equal

tags.name: IllegalArgumentException
tags.content: if obj1 or obj2 are not known to this Comparator and an alternative behavior has not been set via {@link #setUnknownObjectBehavior(UnknownObjectBehavior)}.
tags.description: if obj1 or obj2 are not known to this Comparator and an alternative behavior has not been set via {@link #setUnknownObjectBehavior(UnknownObjectBehavior)}.
